findings: the -17.91%pt regression in the flag-bundled A/B is fully attributable to leaving the bench adapter's safe_mode substring path; the reranker itself changed recall by exactly 0

recall@10 with limit=10 and rerank topK=10 is structurally blind to reranking: reordering the same top-10 set cannot change set membership; adoption judgments need an order-sensitive metric (top1/MRR)
when engaged, the reranker costs ~1.5-3.6s per query on qwen3.5:9b, which rules out default-on for interactive search latency budgets
JSON parse failure rate was 0/15 on the forced-engagement probe (fail-open design returns the original order on failure)
follow_up: if adoption is ever pursued, measure top1/MRR on the real path with a latency budget; recall@10 artifacts cannot decide this
reproducibility: bun run scripts/s154-coding-memory-ab-gate.ts --candidate-env HARNESS_MEM_LLM_RERANK=1 --metric mixed_recall_at_10 --limit 80 (and --candidate-env HARNESS_MEM_BENCH_REAL_PATH=1 for the path-only decomposition)
